feed.title: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
feed.subtitle: Local Rules of the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the District of Maine and their amendment history. Prose/link page with no repeating dated list, monitored by full-page fingerprint so rule amendments surface as change items.

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Added: + Where to File + Video/Telephonic Hearings + Court Holidays + Attorney Reference Guide + Local Rules + - + Links in the Table of Contents, when clicked, will take you to the corresponding rule + The Local Rules were last updated on September 28, 2021 amending Local Bankruptcy Rules 9013-1 and 9013-2. This updated version of the Local Rules includes all amendments through September 28, 2021, and is the official version of the Local Bankruptcy Rules for the District of Maine. + For Interim Rules implementing the Small Business Reorganization Act of 2019, please see the + Standing Order Regarding SBRA Interim Rules + . + Rules Amendments + The process for promulgating an amendment to a rule or form involves several levels of consideration and approval, the first of which is consideration and approval by the Local Rules Committee of the Bankruptcy Court. For a graphic representation of the rules amendment process, please see our + Local Rules Amendments flow chart + . Current local rule amendments published for public comment, if any, appear below. Proposed amendments are published for comment for 30 days. Comments should be sent to + Monica Bigley + , Clerk of Court (please make clear in the subject line of your email that the email is in regard to a proposed local rule). + Revision History + Commencing with revisions made on or after January 1, 2017, all amendments to the local rules (sorted by rule) can be found + here + . + [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Added: + Hearings + Case Assignment + Court Closings + Civility Policy + Credit Reporting Information + CM/ECF Filing Reference Guides + How to Subscribe to the Court's RSS Feed + Pro Bono Honor Roll + Online Payments + Bankruptcy Code + Pending Federal Rule and Form Amendments + Local Rules (effective December 1, 2025) + Summary of Changes to Local Bankruptcy Rules + Kellie Fisher, Chair, Drummond Woodsum, + kfisher@dwmlaw.com + Shawn K. Doil, Verrill, + sdoil@verrill-law.com + Jennifer Hayden, Molleur Law Office, + jen@molleurlaw.com + Nathaniel R. Hull, Verrill, + nhull@verrill-law.com + Anthony Manhart, Perkins Thompson, P.A., + tmanhart@perkinsthompson.com + Ann Dirsa, Office of the U.S. Trustee, + ann.marie.dirsa@usdoj.gov + Local Rules Committee Meetings: + List of Meeting Minutes + Archived Local Rules + Local Rules (effective 1/1/2024 through 12/31/24) + Local Rules (effective 1/1/2025 through 11/30/2025) + Bankruptcy Code + Pending Federal Rule and Form Amendments Removed: - Where to File - Video/Telephonic Hearings - Court Holidays - Attorney Reference Guide - Local Rules - - - Links in the Table of Contents, when clicked, will take you to the corresponding rule - The Local Rules were last updated on September 28, 2021 amending Local Bankruptcy Rules 9013-1 and 9013-2. This updated version of the Local Rules includes all amendments through September 28, 2021, and is the official version [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Added: + Where to File + Video/Telephonic Hearings + Court Holidays + Attorney Reference Guide + Local Rules + - + Links in the Table of Contents, when clicked, will take you to the corresponding rule + The Local Rules were last updated on September 28, 2021 amending Local Bankruptcy Rules 9013-1 and 9013-2. This updated version of the Local Rules includes all amendments through September 28, 2021, and is the official version of the Local Bankruptcy Rules for the District of Maine. + For Interim Rules implementing the Small Business Reorganization Act of 2019, please see the + Standing Order Regarding SBRA Interim Rules + . + Rules Amendments + The process for promulgating an amendment to a rule or form involves several levels of consideration and approval, the first of which is consideration and approval by the Local Rules Committee of the Bankruptcy Court. For a graphic representation of the rules amendment process, please see our + Local Rules Amendments flow chart + . Current local rule amendments published for public comment, if any, appear below. Proposed amendments are published for comment for 30 days. Comments should be sent to + Monica Bigley + , Clerk of Court (please make clear in the subject line of your email that the email is in regard to a proposed local rule). + Revision History + Commencing with revisions made on or after January 1, 2017, all amendments to the local rules (sorted by rule) can be found + here + . + [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.

Doil, Verrill, + sdoil@verrill-law.com + Jennifer Hayden, Molleur Law Office, + jen@molleurlaw.com + Nathaniel R. Hull, Verrill, + nhull@verrill-law.com + Anthony Manhart, Perkins Thompson, P.A., + tmanhart@perkinsthompson.com + Ann Dirsa, Office of the U.S. Trustee, + ann.marie.dirsa@usdoj.gov + Local Rules Committee Meetings: + List of Meeting Minutes + Archived Local Rules + Local Rules (effective 1/1/2024 through 12/31/24) + Local Rules (effective 1/1/2025 through 11/30/2025) + Bankruptcy Code + Pending Federal Rule and Form Amendments Removed: - Where to File - Video/Telephonic Hearings - Court Holidays - Attorney Reference Guide - Local Rules - - - Links in the Table of Contents, when clicked, will take you to the corresponding rule - The Local Rules were last updated on September 28, 2021 amending Local Bankruptcy Rules 9013-1 and 9013-2. This updated version of the Local Rules includes all amendments through September 28, 2021, and is the official version [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Added: + U.S. Court Opinions Removed: - Opinions - 1st Circuit BK Related Opinions
